This is a large building with a white flat roof, resembling a warehouse or shopping center. On one side of the building is Aston Lane road, with a large parking area. On the other side of the building is a railway.

This black colored car is located on the bridge and Walsall Road side at the back of the One Stop building. A semi-circle drawn with a red line marks the entrance to the building from the road between this bridge and the building. The car is parked in the row on the side of this semicircle, with empty parking spaces to its left and right. In front of it is a gray/blue colored car.

At the end of Aldridge Road past Holford Drive on the opposite side of Aldridge, the first building with a brown roof has a sidewalk/alley path running along its side. The house has a raised front yard with two blue trashbins and a pink ball.
The orange-yellow vehicle is parked beside a dark gray-brown building in the center of the Coppice.
This building on Madingley Road has a semi-circular section at the front.
The house near the Braeside building and Castle House is reddish orange and gray.
The house near the Braeside building and Castle House is reddish orange and gray.
The car parked in the disability spot in front of the NT Wholesale Cash & Carry Building, next to the blue car.

The house is located at the corner right end of Orchid street, at the junction between Eden street road and Orchid street road. It has white walls, a white chimney, and a grey roof, with a tree in front of it.
The light gray car is parked in front of the Gray House on Parkside, which is located to the left of the Barr Ellison Solicitors building.
The red car was parked on the side near the intersection of Birchfield Road and Thornbury Road.
Off Parker Street, two red-lined buildings stand - one Brown building with a brown roof and one Base building with a gray roof - connected to the park lodge.
The long brown building is located at the edge of the green grass lawn in front of the left side of the Forbes Mellon Library.
In between two beige buildings with gray roofs is a tree with light green leaves. It is in front of one large tree that covers a section of Eden Street.
The square green ground by the Hillside Building is in the middle of the orange U-shape building.

A silver car parked on the rooftop parking area of a building located on the left side of Parkside police station along Warkworth Terrace road, with a red car parked on its right side and a white car parked on its left side. There are no cars parked behind it.
The two white cars parked in front of the building next to the Barr Ellison Solicitors building on Parkside.
A large parking lot located behind the Arundel House Hotel and a sizable grassfield, which is bordered by the edge of the map, off Chesterton Road.

A silver car with red paint splatters all over it is located in the back lot of a building center on Aston Lane, where the Jobcentre Plus building is situated. It is positioned between a dark vehicle and a partially white vehicle.
The small gray rectangular building located behind the shorter side of the brown building on Mud Lane.
This small parking lot, located on the left side of Castle Street to the left of the 7-18 Northampton Street building, has a dark blue car parked in it.
This red car is positioned in front of a building on Birchfield Road. On one side of the road, there are two white cars, while on the other side, there are two black cars and a blue panel van.

A black car is parked on the pavement below the white car park building. It is on the right pavement in between the parkside police station and the car park building. It is near the building wall and has free parking spaces on the left, back, and front.
The massive rectangular green lawn is located adjacent to the long side of the North Paddock.
Next to Angel & Blume building, there is a long building with white walls along Orchard Street road, with a golden green tree behind it.

On Aldridge Road, directly across from the intersection with Holford Road, there are two large buildings. Just beyond these, there are several residential buildings with red tiled roofs. The building in question is the first part of the second residential building with a red roof and has a trampoline in the back garden.

construction.identity_feature: first part of the second residential building, has a trampoline in the back garden

description: A silver car parked on the rooftop parking area of a building located on the left side of Parkside police station along Warkworth Terrace road, with a red car parked on its right side and a white car parked on its left side. There are no cars parked behind it.
Off Bragg Road, the right side of a white house with a gray roof features an entirely cement backyard.
The Scholar's Lawn is across the water from the large green lawn.

A square, stone building on Parkside, the third from the corner of Claredon, with a gray roof that has multiple hipped sections that are white at the gables. The building also has two round roof tower sections, with one in the center featuring triangular skylight windows.

construction.identity_feature: square, stone structure, multiple hipped roof sections, two round roof towers, one with triangular skylight windows

description: The parking lot behind the long side of the Brewhouse is gray.
The gray house on Chesterton Lane behind the Saint Giles building.
Near the edge of the map, on Grafton Street, there is a white multi-unit housing complex with a gray roof next to a beige building with a brown roof that has solar panels on it.
The parking lot bordered by the Perry Barr footpath is triangular.

In a small 6-car parking lot along Warkworth Terrace, a black car is parked next to another black car. The small parking lot is located in front of Cambridge & Peterborough Probation Trust. There is an empty parking spot on one side of the car and another black car on the other side (to its left).
This gray car is third from the left in the row of nine cars parked to the south of West Midlands Police Custody Suite.
Along Aldridge road, there is a building with a brown roof and white walls. In front of the building, there is a small square-shaped lawn with a black car parked in front of it.
At the end of Wellington Road near Leslie Road, a long gray building stands beside a blue long building.
The building that is just above the La Mimosa building is big, brown, and roofed.
The green patch of land is located between Castlebrae and Castle House on the left side of an alley leading to Castle House.
A multi-colored building in gray, white, and beige, featuring a gray roof and five rows of skylights, located off Warkworth Terrace next to the Parkside police station, near the edge of the map.

description: On the edge of the map, across from the Garden Court Building, a t-shaped beige building with a gray roof sits on the corner of Prospect Row and Adam and Eve Street.

On the side of Willmore Road stands a tall building with a gray roof. The roof features 8 chimneys arranged in 4 rows, with two chimneys per row. Behind the building, there are 4 gardens, each enclosed by walls. The middle garden contains a yellow tree.

description: The building on the corner of Eden Street Backway and Portland Place has a gray roof with evenly spaced chimneys and small attached buildings with slanted roofs that look sawtoothed.

description: A red car is parked on Chesterton Road between a white van and another red car. The white van is in front of the car, and the other red car is behind it. There is a tree diagonally behind the car.
The blue car is parked between the black car and the white car at the parking lot behind the One Stop Building next to the Walsall Road, second entry and it is on your right.

A red car is parked between a blue car and a white car near the end of the row of cars in the parking lot on the corner of Wellington Road and Station Bridge. A dark red van is parked perpendicular to it a few cars away.
On Thornbury Road, across from the words "School Keep Clean" etched into the road, there is a gray roofed house near two dark blue cars.
The building to the left of the Merton House building is tiny and grayish brown.
This is a house on Thornbury Road with no cars in front of it. On the opposite side of the road, there is a rectangle of grass, with the school keep clear markings starting in front of it as well.

The building, with three gable sections on it, is located on the right side of the intersection between Birchfield Road, which runs horizontally below, and Willmore Road, which runs vertically just left of the building. There are two black cars below the building.
The two white cars parked in front of the building next to the Barr Ellison Solicitors building on Parkside.
The parking lot in a C-shape encircles the Wellington house along East Road.
On the side of Aldridge Road is a building, which is the first of three similar buildings on the roadside. Next to this building is the W. D. M. Cars building.

This red car is parked on the top floor of a multi-storey car park located between the Cambridge & Peterborough Probation Trust building and the Police Station buildings. It is the first car in a row of 3 red cars, situated next to the Police Station building.
A one-floor white building with a multi-colored gray and white roof is located between the tufnol building and the railroad.
On the side of Aldridge Road is a building, which is the first of three similar buildings on the roadside. Next to this building is the W. D. M. Cars building.

On the side of Willmore Road stands a tall building with a gray roof. The roof features 8 chimneys arranged in 4 rows, with two chimneys per row. Behind the building, there are 4 gardens, each enclosed by walls. The middle garden contains a yellow tree.

description: This red car is parked in the middle top row of the parking lot, seventh car from the left, between Wellington Road and Station Bridge.
The parking lot behind the long side of the Brewhouse is gray.
Within the new Square and Emmanuel Road city blocks, there is a large grassfield with a footpath running diagonally across it and a row of trees also running diagonally near the edge of the map.
This red car is in the southmost row of the parking lot, seventh car from the right, between Wellington Road and Station Bridge.
Near the intersection with Birchfield Road on The Broadway, there is a magenta car along with a small blue vehicle behind it.

In the parking lot of a multicolored beige and brown building with a gray roof on the corner of Birchfield Road and Wellington Road near the roundabout, there is a black car parked in the center row between two red cars, with a red car behind it.

This small square building with a red roof is located in the middle of the buildings between City Road and Eden Street, not on any street side. Surrounding the building are trees on three sides and a footpath on one side. Next to one of the trees is a small black tree and a large building with 10 skylights on its roof.
Next to the Perry Barr Carpets building off Bragg Road, there is a left section of a house with a brown roof. In front of the house, there is one white car and one blue car.

This building has two stories and red walls. It is located on the opposite side of Chesterwood Road, not at the dead end, and is the last building on this side of the road. Its roof is not adjacent to neighboring buildings. There is a blue, round door or window-like section on the side of the building next to it.
There is a white car on Victoria Street across the road and diagonal from Christ's Court, in the direction towards Clarendon Street. There is a white car in front of it and a red car behind it.
The large parking lot off of Chessterton Road between the Staff House and the Arundel House Hotel.
This white and gray roofed building, located between Fisher Building (St John's College) and Bin Brook, features a blue and white window-like object near its center.
The small gray building is behind the large long gray building on Aston Lane, across the street from the NT Wholesale Cash & Carry.
The green patch of land is located between Castlebrae and Castle House on the left side of an alley leading to Castle House.
In front of the blue car parked on a path stemming from Birchfield Road is a gray car, and behind it is a yellow car with a black roof.
The blue building, which appears as an inverted "E" when viewed from Eden Street Backway road horizontally, is situated with Portland Place road running vertically to its left.

At the end of the first row of cars parked against the building from the entry direction of the parking area beside the One Stop, there is a red car. It is between two darker colored cars and there is a marked out area with yellow hatchmarks in front of it.
The buildings on Clarendon Street include the one on the corner of Earl Street, the one with a bright white roof, and the next one with two cars in front of it.
A section of the large building with a brown roof located off Hertford Street, adjacent to the Marino Place building, facing the intersection of Hertford Street and East Hertford Street.
The irregularly shaped parking lot is located next to the Parkside gym building, behind the large rectangular brown building near the Melbourne place footpath.
A rectangular ground next to the white roofed Manchester and Bournemouth station that is well manned.
Several long and skinny whitish brown buildings with gray pitched roofs are situated between Prospect Row and Brandon Place. Some of the buildings form a courtyard within the complex.

The building at the end of Holford Drive is large, white, and has many solar panels on top. It also has three parking lots.

description: A red car is parked on Chesterton Road between a white van and another red car. The white van is in front of the car, and the other red car is behind it. There is a tree diagonally behind the car.
The green patch of land is located between Castlebrae and Castle House on the left side of an alley leading to Castle House.
The building on Thornbury Road next to a school is dark brown with a dark brown roof.
The structure located on The Broadway road features a red car parked in front of it. It has a brown and gray roof, while the neighboring property has a white car.
Behind a brown-roofed building on Castle Street is a building, with a parking space nearby that has a few cars in it.

The construction equipment is a brown object with a blue section in the middle. It is located in a large area of brown dirt behind the NT Wholesale Cash & Carry building, and is the vehicle closest to the building.
The building to the left of the Merton House building is tiny and grayish brown.
This plot of land in front of Mallory Court has a small parking space with a few cars nearby, and is connected to Castle Street.
The rectangular lawn that is bordered by Park Terrace is large and green.
Next to the Perry Barr carpets building, off Bridgefield Road, is a multi-building complex of various colors, sizes, and shapes.
The building to the left of the Merton House building is tiny and grayish brown.
The first of four identical brown buildings on Burton Wood Drive coming from Livingston Road, with a gray car parked in the driveway behind the house.
The dark brown 'c'-shaped building, located to the left of Thornbury Road when viewed from above and situated above the blue rectangular ground.

Viewed from above with Birchfield Road on the right, the bright blue car was parked alone in the far right corner of the parking lot, behind the brown building at the intersection of Birchfield Road and Wellington Road.
A red car is parked on Earl Street with a white car in front and another white car behind it, all in the right lane. Across the street, in the opposite lane, there is a black car.

The building on Bragg Road, fourth in line, is close to the access road that goes behind the big building on the corner. Behind it, there is a yellow van. The building has a white front and a gray roof, with a white car parked in front of it.
This residential house on Bridgelands Way has a few solar panels on its roof and three cars parked nearby.

construction.identity_feature: residential, has solar panels on roof

description: The small gray building is behind the large long gray building on Aston Lane, across the street from the NT Wholesale Cash & Carry.
In the parking lot of the Arundel House Hotel, a small yellow car and a white car are parked two spaces away from each other.
Off Parker Street, two red-lined buildings stand - one Brown building with a brown roof and one Base building with a gray roof - connected to the park lodge.
The building on the corner of Eden Street Backway and Portland Place has a gray roof with evenly spaced chimneys and small attached buildings with slanted roofs that look sawtoothed.

construction.identity_feature: evenly spaced chimneys, small attached buildings with slanted sawtoothed roofs

description: Located on Warkworth Terrace at the corner where it meets Warkworth Street, there is a light stone colored building with two turrets at the front and a few trees around it. Additionally, there are cars on the curb out front, including two red ones at the corner.

construction.identity_feature: with two turrets at the front

description: This gray building, located on the left side of Emmanuel Road, is directly north of the Angel & Blume building and to the right of a yellowing tree.
The house is facing directly to the white patch in the rectangular empty compound on the opposite side of Thornbury Road, in the long chain of houses on the right.
There is a rectangular building with a white wall on the right lane along City Road. In front of the building, there is a tree. On the opposite lane, there is John Street road.
This long rectangular building is located on Eden Street back way and Portland Place. The roof, which is gray, has six chimneys. Behind the building, there are several trees.
The green playing fields and tennis courts off of Queen's Road are marked with white painted lines.
The section of a multi housing complex off Bayswater Road with a multicolored brown and gray roof is located at the corner of Bayswater Road and Canterbury Road.

In the center left section of a multi-colored housing complex, there is a red-lined area with a white car parked next to a black car. The complex extends from the edge of the map to a multicolored red and brown building with a gray roof. This section is located across from a small rectangular Grassfield on a playground off Thornbury Road.

construction.identity_feature: multicolored red and brown, gray roof

description: The gray houses on Willmore Road, located to the right of the backyard at the intersection with Birchfield Road.

This is a large building with a white flat roof, resembling a warehouse or shopping center. On one side of the building is Aston Lane road, with a large parking area. On the other side of the building is a railway.
This building, located next to Fitzroy Street, sits on Fitzroy Street Backway. It has a light gray roof with four sections, each T-ing into the next section.

construction.identity_feature: roof with four sections, each T-ing into the next section

description: The blue car, located second from the bottom in the rightmost column of cars in the parking lot of the One Stop Building, when viewed from above with Walsall Road positioned below.
The building with a brown roof that is white and located on Warkworth Terrace Road.
The brown house on Aldridge Road, located to the right of the W. D. M. Cars building.
A maroon car parked near the front of the parking lot behind the building at Wellington Road and Birchfield Road between a light blue car and a light gray car.
Behind the homes on Willmore Road is a very large, long, rectangular building with a flat gray roof at the bottom of the sloped white roof, which has a line of windows at the top.

construction.identity_feature: very large, long, rectangular, flat roof at bottom, sloped roof above, line of windows at top

description: The red car is the 11th from the left in the 2nd row of cars in the parking lot behind the brown building at the intersection of Birchfield Road and Wellington Road, as viewed aerially with Birchfield Road to the right.
A multi-colored building in gray, white, and beige, featuring a gray roof and five rows of skylights, located off Warkworth Terrace next to the Parkside police station, near the edge of the map.

construction.identity_feature: multi-colored, gray roof, five rows of skylights

description: The row of gray houses on New Square to the left of the New Square Dental & Chiropody building.
The red car is parked in the middle row of the parking lot on the corner of Wellington Road and Station Road. It is between a black car and a white car, with a white car parked behind it.
The building with a brown roof that is white and located on Warkworth Terrace Road.
A long, rectangular olive-colored house situated between the NT Wholesale Cash & Carry building and the railroad.
To the left of La Mimosa, this gray roofed house is the leftmost one between St John's Road and Park Parade.
The red car is parked between blue and white cars on the third lane from the West Midlands Police Custody Suite.
A black car is parked alongside the short side of a brown building on Adam and Eve Street.
The big empty parking lot area, shaped like a rectangle, is found above the Orchard Court building and below the big fancy building above it.

A red car is parked between a blue car and a white car near the end of the row of cars in the parking lot on the corner of Wellington Road and Station Bridge. A dark red van is parked perpendicular to it a few cars away.

A parking lot next to the Edwards court building is buying a page building with a brown roof, which is also next to a set of three homes with gray roofs and the castle End building with a light blue roof.

The red car is parked in the last row of the parking lot at the corner building located at the intersection of Station Bridge Road and Wellington Road. It is situated between a grey car on the right and another grey car on the left, with a green tree behind it.
The front section of a brown building with a light brown roof that has three chimneys on it is located on the edge of the map next to the Grassfield triangle, off Castle Street.
This small gray roofed building is located on the south side of Orchard Street, across from the Angel & Blume building.
The brown house on Aldridge Road, located to the right of the W. D. M. Cars building.
The parking lot in a C-shape encircles the Wellington house along East Road.
This gray building, located on the left side of Emmanuel Road, is directly north of the Angel & Blume building and to the right of a yellowing tree.
The rectangular parking lot located adjacent to the Barr Ellison Solicitors building, situated at the corner of the Warkworth Terrace footpath.
A warehouse with a white roof located on Birchfield Road, situated between Thornbury and Canterbury.
The light gray car is parked in front of the Gray House on Parkside, which is located to the left of the Barr Ellison Solicitors building.
When looking across Aldrige Road from Holden Drive, this house is to the left. At the front of the house, you will see two blue trash bins positioned between two green bins.
The gray house at the intersection of Earl Street and Clarendon Street.
